thanks for that so I started doing this
in in January of 2020 and we used to
really focus on the third Friday this
being spot gam by the way you're you're
SP spot gam yeah I have I'm sad to say
not sad but I don't want to age myself
but I have over 20 years of
institutional experience worked at
several different banks and options
Market maker and a family office so 20
years of experience but I started spot
gamma officially in January of of 2020
and we used to talk about the options
flows tied to the monthly expirations
right and there was this cycle and you
could really kind of set your watch to
the flows around the cycle and kind of
forecast the impact of that and you kind
of fast forward to now zero DT options a
lot of people talked about you probably
say brand I'm tired of hearing this but
the fact is that 55% of SPX index
options are zero DTE now and that's
billions of dollars of hedging flows day
in and day out related to these
positions and these positions will be
there today and then they will be gone
tomorrow for example there's this big
Iron Condor trade we talk about all the
time and if you watch where those legs
are know Market will move or respond to
those strikes and that that happens on a
daily basis right um so the the cycle of
flows that I watch has become again much
shorter term in nature and then I
mentioned the The Leverage before and
what I think the value of that now for
people who have a longer term view is
you get these price dislocations now
that can be extreme and it's not every
day but once a month at least if not
more often you see some type of extreme
price dislocation either in the market
itself or in an in a major single stock
I'll give you an
example three weeks ago two weeks ago
was the Nvidia deep seek news right
Nvidia had as I'm sure you talked about
the biggest one-day market cap loss ever
I think that was the 27th is that right
yeah uh over $500 billion of market cap
loss stock was down
16% uh in a day right and you know if
you look at that actual deep seek
headline the news was kind of out before
that already like it wasn't necessarily
anything new and you kind of fast
forward and it's like well Nvidia now
stock has almost kind of rebounded from
that sort of level so like what was that
and the thing that I think is
interesting is if you look at Nvidia it
has it is the number one largest option
single stock that there is there's more
open interest in that stock than any
other single stock second only to the
SPX index in terms of notional values
trading second thing is there are
billions upon billions of dollars of
levered ETFs in Nvidia like the Nvidia
2x long ETF for example so when Nvidia
goes down 5% not only do you have these
options flows which are leveraged right
uh because of the of the hedging nature
but then you have these ETFs which are
hedged in a variety of different ways
but you see these headlines come out
nowadays that say Hey you know such and
such ETF can't get any more swap lines
from their Banks because the assets
grown too big right and the assets flood
into these things and flood out so when
you get a move I'm and I'm just kind of
saying this as an anecdote right maybe
Nvidia should have only been down 3% on
that news but it ends up down 10%
because of this leverage and then people
see that 10% move and they go oh my gosh
this deep seek news must be really
important because Nvidia stock is down
10% when in fact it's just the leverage
right that is driving the price
dislocation so when I look at a lot of
these names that are moving a lot you
can see it's often times interesting to
compare the underlying volatilities on
these things and see hey V is not really
moving in sync or V is kind of jumping
more than we expect but we know that
there's these giant flows that are
exacerbating these price moves so the
kind of soap box I've been standing on
lately is you probably heard the axium
you know uh the news doesn't matter it's
the reaction to the news that matters
right well now it's almost kind of like
the opposite it's like the reaction to
the news often times is so overbaked and
overdone that because of this leverage
that people could then go the Deep seek
news must oh my gosh like like the
United States economy like put a fork in
it like let's all go you know start
paying youan for stuff now because
China's got us by the you know um you
know what so and it turns out like you
fast forward a couple weeks it's like no
that's not actually not really true
right so you know that's the kind of
things that happened and and so that's
just Nvidia but you can look at the same
thing in terms of uh you know you look
at like pal teers had this incredible
move up and the ball gets really
stretched to the upside Tesla's been
another one where after the election the
amount of call options in that in that
stock went bananas right and and B
spikes and the name doubles and I think
people go well you Tesla must be firing
on all cylinders uh excuse the pun and
and they're not it's just like there's
this extra options leverage that's in
the system so for people I think that
have that longer term view knowing that
these leverag positions can create these
significant price dislocations maybe you
want to be a buyer of of the dip in
there maybe you just want to think twice
about selling because the Paradigm
really didn't change because it's just
this leverage that is exacerbating
movement right it's like a margin call
right if someone's getting a margin call
and they're puking you're generally
happy going to be on their side of that
stock right because you know they're
forced out of it and they're and they're
causing that short-term price

that something's coming your way do you

the think you know the the markets and

across time Horizons you have different

different asset classes that inherently
have different catalysts that are
generally speaking you know have signal

kind of certainly an Institutional

you know B correct me if I'm wrong but

because we have a lot more inherent
leverage in the system that is
concentrated shorter duration we have a
higher level of realized volatility uh
especially in response to news than we
used to have and what that ultimately
means is we probably have more
short-term Priceless locations relative
to underlying fundamentals medium to
longer term fundamentals and so it
probably makes it more lucrative for
investors to concentrate themselves in a
Time Horizon buckets you know if you're

prices locations and and and and and

know you put it so well uh as you always

if you saw the S&P like we've had three
separate instances of this roughly in
the last two weeks S&P has gone from
6,100 to 6,000 right in the matter of
like an hour like we'll we'll
just like it's a liquidity hole like we
just drop right there's no bid it's just
down we go so we'll have a two two% drop
right one to two% drop and realized all
when you look at a month doesn't budge
right there's no reaction to that at all
normally what you would expect is when

that you would see longer dated Vols
lift you would see positions shift a

there would be a little bit of
stickiness to that to that jump um but

just just not there to this point you
know CPI this morning S&P was down uh
Futures down a percent S&P is ried all
the way back and you look at one month
falls out two three months and they
haven't budged from like 133% right so
there's no reaction of that at all and I
think some of that is you have these
short dated positions that are either
piling on bringing mean reversion what
whatever it is they're impacting Market
but there's like this stable point one
month out that's not really shifting and
the only time you really seem to see
that stable Point shift is when there's
like an obvious unknown that comes in
right um think of like the Silicon
Valley Bank crisis for example it's like
we all know the banks are risk you know
having some risk here banks are failing
more Banks might fail we don't know
which ones and we don't know when it is
I can't hedge that with the zero DTE or
onee option I got to buy you know like a
three-month put for example to hedge
myself on that scenario so it's those
like obvious long-term threats that seem
to lift fall and present some risks to
the system but you know without that uh
people seem to be able to hedge out kind
of idiosyncratic risk like a you know
fed statement or a CPI with these short
dated options you get a little bit
jumpiness in the short dated options but
things ultimately just sort of mean seem
to mean revert in that in the long St
longterm EVs and and and options prices

risk management overlay to you know send

its proper trade recommendations across
the 70 um factors that ATT tracks and
the the Genesis for making that that

trend isn't changing and so what I don't
want is to have a systematic process
that generates a high frequency of false
alarms because the trend isn't changing
and so I essentially May slowed the
whole entire process down so that it can
have a little bit more elasticity in the

accelerate over the next few months

Equity valuations right to me the market
is shrugging off bad news on the Tariff
front for a reason uh and one of those
reasons is the fact that we hit the debt
cell right the treasury was trying to
borrow $816 billion of net new
marketable borrowing here in q1 they
can't do that so that's $816 billion
that now needs to either stay where it
was or go find home somewhere right and
so my opinion I think that's one of
those super supportive Dynamics as well
again it's not all great news there's
other things that are on the negative
side that can cause some problems if I
mean we could get a really nasty
headline from Trump what if reciprocal
terorists are even worse than what we
think they are yes could be it could be
very negative but the reality is in my
opinion I think I learned this from

upside capture bull markets and minimize

think the Market's going to crash in the

in Nvidia we see equivalent moves in
names like this now to the upside as

period it was up 50% in a matter of like

levels like what we're looking at now
where they're so rich that for Baba to
maintain the price momentum needed to
make these calls pay off starts to
become like statistically like just
super improbable right like there's no
definites in this game obviously as you
well know but you start to go okay this
makes sense tactically to take some off

or sell a little bit of upside risk to
you know monetize that that with some
call selling uh and then when that VA
comes back in you close up your calls
and you still have your asset right um
so so so those are some of the things

for sure I mean and and the reason is
because the short dated options react
faster so this is term structuring gold
and those shorter dated implied balls
are always going to be more sensitive
right so they should shift to more
convex nature more quickly particularly
now in the days of call Demand right um
you know there it's one thing for the

bunch of moves so there's like a new
realized VA that has to get kind of
priced in or that price momentum has to
get priced in uh and skew would shift
off of that but the second one is Demand
right if people start buying tons of
calls that's going to shift that skew up
because as as that demand comes in
prices have to rise and and market
makers can protect themselves against
upside Risk by charging more for the
calls right so it's like as a market

suggests that dealers and market makers

um and I think in that this Market's
really you know that's being reinforced
right um you know to the point of buying
options because I think they're cheap or
you know selling them because I think
they're expensive um if you pay too much
attention to the noise you can get
shaken out of that I think pretty easily
and often times that's wrong so um I'm
really just kind of focused on on that
right it's almost like get off of the
Twitter get off of the noise and and pay
attention to price and you know if you
buy things at which you think are
reasonable prices then you're going to
do well and if you pay too much uh off
of an emotional kind of overshoot then
then you're going to get tagged so I
love it yeah it's basic but that seems
to be really you know what it is um I'll
add a secondary point to that Brent
which is you know our entire in our
entire risk management process 100% of
the asset allocation and portfolio
construction recommendations that we
make for our clients are a function of
our price and volatility tools right we
don't make you know fundamentally
oriented risk management decisions like
oh buy the treasury bond because
inflation's slowing or or bu stocks
because growth's accelerating we don't
make those calls anymore you know you
know year or last two plus years now and
our track record over the last two plus
years has you know been up there with
anybody in my opinion because absolutely
I've seen that markets have trended more
towards being price and volatility
machines and momentum machines and and
the thing I will say I'll leave I'll
leave investors with and I actually love
your your take on this before we go I
think in a world where we have more AI
informing investors and ultimately
making investment decisions our ability
as investors to connect the dot between
tomorrow's price and some fundamental
Catalyst that we believe in or some
narrative that we construct in our head
our ability to do that will go down
because the AI will make you know they
will just be better at that than us
right know that's that's kind of the

have systems that are taking advantage
of momentum and inflections in momentum
and asset markets in terms of price and
volatility terms that are weaved into
your risk management process I think
you're going to struggle as an investor
as we move forward in time you know with
